title: melaleuca acuminata subsp. websteri
intro: Melaleuca acuminata subsp. websteri, also known as the Western or Western Swamp Paperbark, is a subspecies of the Melaleuca acuminata plant native to Western Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of volatile oils. The health benefits of Melaleuca acuminata subsp. websteri include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious infections.
